title: Tapping to Let Safety Set the Tone at Night
subtitle: A bedtime tapping practice for signalling to the body that peace is safe and nothing is coming
excerpt: A five-minute bedtime tapping practice from Day 28 of a 30-day sleep programme. The script works with the body's habit of bracing for something to go wrong even in quiet moments — the vigilance that makes peace feel unfamiliar. The reframe trains the nervous system to read stillness as protection rather than warning.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from the Day 28 night tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use in bed at night when the body is still scanning for threat in the quiet — when stillness feels precarious rather than safe, or when the nervous system stays alert even after the day is done.

content.text: A five-minute bedtime tapping practice from Day 28 of a 30-day sleep programme. The night tap for Day 28 works with the vigilance that can persist even when the external situation is safe — the body scanning for a reason to tense up, unable to fully stand down. The reframe trains a new signal: stillness means protection, not danger.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though my body still expects something to go wrong, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though peace can feel foreign, I honour that it's becoming more familiar.
Even though I'm new to sleeping without worry, I'm open to letting safety feel normal.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I'm not waiting for a reason to tense up.
Side of eye: Nothing's coming — it's okay.
Under eye: I breathe into the quiet and let it settle me.
Under nose: My body can stand down completely.
Chin: Stillness means protection now, not danger.
Collarbone: I'm surrounded by calm.
Under arm: I'm safe to sleep without standing guard.
Top of head: My body understands peace tonight.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I rest knowing I'm secure.
Side of eye: Safety wraps around me as I settle.
Under eye: My system trusts the stillness.
Under nose: I relax into a stable rhythm.
Chin: I'm at ease in predictability.
Collarbone: Safety feels like home now.
Under arm: I'm safe, sound, and steady.
Top of head: I sleep peacefully in trust.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from the Day 28 night tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
